meaning: adj.能养活的,能生育的,可行的
examples: The plane will be commercially viable if 400 can be sold.
If industrial agriculture is to be replaced, what is a viable alternative?
These farmers will never actually sell any of the lands, provided that farming it remains viable.
And Hanks, while undeniably bankable, is, at 50, no longer viable for most leading-man scripts.
Critics applauded the effort, but said the government should do more to implement viable solutions.
